perturbation.original_text: Heritage shall produce the products described on Schedule A and Schedule A-1 attached hereto, as may be amended by the Parties hereafter from time to time (the "Products," each individual unit of Product "Unit"), for Premier at [***] Heritage's or Heritage's wholly owned subsidiary, Jasper's facilities (the "Facilities"). [***], except that, notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, [***].
perturbation.changed_text: Heritage shall produce the products described on Schedule A and Schedule A-1 attached hereto, as may be amended by the Parties hereafter from time to time (the "Products"), for Premier at [***] Heritage's or Heritage's wholly owned subsidiary, Jasper's facilities (the "Facilities"). [***], except that, notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, [***].
perturbation.explanation: By removing the definition of 'Unit' as 'each individual unit of Product', the contract becomes ambiguous. While it refers to 'Products', the specific definition clarifying what constitutes a 'Unit' of Product is now missing. This creates an in-text contradiction since subsequent clauses referring to 'Units' (as seen in section 2(b), 2(f) and 2(g)) now lack a clear definition, making it difficult to determine the basis for pricing and quantity calculations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2(c) of the Agreement is amended so that the term "Units" as defined therein is now referred to as "Tetra Units".
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the definition of 'Unit', which is 'each individual unit of Product'. The contradicted text refers to 'Units' as defined in Section 2(c), which is amended to now be referred to as 'Tetra Units'. However, the original definition of 'Unit' is missing, which means that the term 'Tetra Unit' also lacks a clear definition, creating a direct contradiction in how the agreement handles quantities and descriptions of items.

perturbation.original_text: Heritage shall purchase all ingredients and packaging materials identified in the relevant Schedule C or Schedule C-l to be used in connection with the manufacturer of the Products. Heritage shall invoice Premier through the [***] billing as identified on the relevant Schedule C or Schedule C-l. Heritage shall not, however, purchase ingredients or packaging materials in excess of those required [***].
perturbation.changed_text: Heritage shall purchase all ingredients and packaging materials identified in the relevant Schedule C or Schedule C-l to be used in connection with the manufacturer of the Products. Heritage shall invoice Premier through the [***] billing. Heritage shall not, however, purchase ingredients or packaging materials in excess of those required [***].
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to Schedule C or C-1 from the billing clause, it is unclear which schedule governs the billing process. Given that Schedule C and C-1 outline pricing and terms for different product types, the omission creates uncertainty as to which pricing structure applies for invoicing. This omission regarding the Schedule C and C-1 creates an in-text contradiction due to the fact that section 3(b) specifies that ingredients and packaging materials should be specified on Schedule C or C-1. However the subsequent line no longer specifies which Schedule should be used for Billing
perturbation.contradicted_text: Heritage shall purchase all ingredients and packaging materials identified in the relevant Schedule C or Schedule C-l to be used in connection with the manufacturer of the Products. Heritage shall invoice Premier through the [***] billing.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that invoices should be processed according to the billing identified in Schedule C or C-l. The changed text removes the reference to these schedules. The contradicted text is the changed text which shows the contradiction. As the explanation states, given that Schedule C and C-1 outline pricing and terms for different product types, the omission creates uncertainty as to which pricing structure applies for invoicing. This uncertainty creates a direct conflict because the contradicted text no longer specifies which schedule should be used for Billing. Thus, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered by Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall conform to the specifications supplied to Heritage by Premier as listed on Schedule A and/or Schedule A-1, which Schedules may from time to time be modified by Premier in writing (the "Specifications"), shall conform to Post Holding's Quality Expectations Manual attached hereto as Schedule E, and shall conform in all material respects to samples previously supplied to Premier by Heritage.
perturbation.changed_text: All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered by Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall conform to the specifications supplied to Heritage by Premier as listed on Schedule A and/or Schedule A-1, which Schedules may from time to time be modified by Premier in writing (the "Specifications"), and shall conform in all material respects to samples previously supplied to Premier by Heritage.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for products to conform to Post Holding's Quality Expectations Manual (Schedule E), the contract creates an in-text contradiction. While the products still need to meet the specifications listed in Schedule A and A-1, the absence of Schedule E compliance introduces ambiguity, especially if other clauses in the agreement (or related documents) reference or rely on the Quality Expectations Manual. This removal makes it hard to determine the quality standards the product must meet, creating an in-text contradiction with Post Holdings quality expectations previously mentioned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered by Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall conform to the specifications supplied to Heritage by Premier as listed on Schedule A and/or Schedule A-1, which Schedules may from time to time be modified by Premier in writing (the "Specifications"), and shall conform in all material respects to samples previously supplied to Premier by Heritage.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires adherence to both the specifications in Schedules A and A-1 AND the Post Holding's Quality Expectations Manual (Schedule E). The changed text removes the explicit requirement to conform to Schedule E, which creates a contradiction because the agreement now lacks a clearly defined set of quality standards. If other sections of the contract rely on the Quality Expectations Manual, the removal introduces ambiguity and the potential for disputes regarding quality compliance, especially since the Contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction under the 'Omissions' definition.
